searchDocs.title: Connection​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Connection { Connected: boolean-- Whether the connection is still active. Disconnect: (self: Connection) → ()-- Disconnects and deactivates this connection. } A live connection returned by Signal:Connect.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal.new() → Signal<T...> Creates a new Signal. local signal = Signal.new()

searchDocs.title: Connect​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Connect( fn: (T...) → ()-- Callback to invoke when the signal fires. ) → Connection Connects a callback to this signal. Returns a Connection that can be used to disconnect later. local c = signal:Connect(function(value) print(value) end) -- later: c:Disconnect()

searchDocs.title: Fire​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Fire( ...: T...-- Arguments forwarded to all connected callbacks. ) → () Fires the signal, calling all connected callbacks with the provided arguments.

searchDocs.title: Once​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Once( fn: (T...) → ()-- Callback to invoke once. ) → Connection Connects a callback that automatically disconnects after firing once.

searchDocs.title: Wait​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Signal:Wait() → T... Yields the current thread until the signal fires, then returns the fired arguments. local winner = gm.Stopped:Wait()

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Destroy() → () Disconnects all connections and makes the signal inert.

searchDocs.title: useState​
searchDocs.content: </> HookContext:useState( initial: T-- Value used only on the first call. ) → ( T,-- Current value. Dep<T>-- Callable setter. Also has a :Connect method to observe changes. ) Returns the current value of a persistent state slot and a Dep that can be called to update it. On subsequent calls the initial value is ignored and the stored value is returned. gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local score, setScore = ctx:useState(0) -- call setScore(n) to update end)

searchDocs.title: useReducer​
searchDocs.content: </> HookContext:useReducer( reducer: ( state: T, action: A ) → T,-- Pure function that computes the next state. initial: T-- Initial state value. ) → ( T,-- Current state. Dep<A>-- Callable dispatcher. ) Like useState but the setter accepts an action that is passed through a reducer function to produce the next state. local function reducer(state, action) if action == "increment" then return state + 1 end return state end gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local count, dispatch = ctx:useReducer(reducer, 0) dispatch("increment") end)

searchDocs.title: useMemo​
searchDocs.content: </> HookContext:useMemo( fn: () → T,-- Function that computes the value. deps: {any}-- List of values to watch for changes. ) → T-- Cached computed value. Returns a cached value that is only recomputed when one of the deps changes between calls. gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local expensive = ctx:useMemo(function() return computeSomething(self.Score) end, { self.Score }) end)

searchDocs.title: useCallback​
searchDocs.content: </> HookContext:useCallback( fn: T,-- Function to memoize. deps: {any}-- List of values to watch for changes. ) → T-- Stable function reference. Returns a memoized function reference that only changes when one of the deps changes. Equivalent to useMemo(function() return fn end, deps).

searchDocs.title: useRef​
searchDocs.content: </> HookContext:useRef( initial: T-- Initial value of .current. ) → Ref<T> Returns a persistent mutable ref object with a .current field. Unlike useState, mutating .current does not trigger a re-run. gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local timerRef = ctx:useRef(0) timerRef.current += dt end)

searchDocs.title: useEffect​
searchDocs.content: </> HookContext:useEffect( fn: () → (() → ())?,-- Effect function. Return a cleanup function or nothing. deps: {any}-- Re-runs the effect when any dep changes. ) → () Runs a side effect when deps change. The effect function may return a cleanup function that is called before the next run or when the mount is destroyed. gm:mount(function(self, ctx) ctx:useEffect(function() local c = self.Stopped:Connect(function() print("stopped") end) return function() c:Disconnect() end end, {}) end)

searchDocs.title: TeamInfo​
searchDocs.content: </> interface TeamInfo { Points: number-- Accumulated score toward PointsNeeded. Capturing: number-- Current capture progress from 0 to CaptureTime. inZoneCount: number-- Number of eligible players currently in the zone. isHolder: boolean-- Whether this team is currently a holder. } Per-team state tracked each round.

searchDocs.title: Settings​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Settings { Teams: {Team }-- Teams competing in the round. Required. Parts: {BasePart }-- Parts that define the capture zone. Required. Increase: number?-- Points per second earned while holding. Defaults to 1. Rollback: number?-- Points per second lost while not holding. Defaults to 1. PointsNeeded: number?-- Points required to win. Defaults to 1200. RequireDominanceToIncrease: boolean?-- Only earn points when opponent has zero. Defaults to true. ProgressWhileContested: boolean?-- Allow capture progress when both teams are present. Defaults to true. CaptureWhileDead: boolean?-- Count dead players toward zone occupation. Defaults to false. CaptureStacking: boolean?-- Scale capture speed by player count difference. Defaults to false. CaptureTime: number?-- Seconds to fully capture the point. Defaults to 1. OvertimeThreshold: number?-- Elapsed seconds before overtime begins. -1 disables. Defaults to -1. OvertimeIncrease: number?-- Points per second during overtime. Defaults to 1. OvertimeRollback: number?-- Rollback rate during overtime. Defaults to 1. OvertimePointsNeeded: number?-- Points to win during overtime. Defaults to 120. OvertimeDefenderWinFullDominance: boolean?-- Defenders win if fully captured while opponent has zero. Defaults to true. DefendingTeam: Team? -- The defending team for overtime. Defaults to Teams[1]. } Configuration passed to Terminal.new.

searchDocs.title: ContestedChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.ContestedChanged: Signal<boolean> Fires when the zone transitions between contested and uncontested.

searchDocs.title: OvertimeEntered​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.OvertimeEntered: Signal<> Fires when the round transitions into overtime.

searchDocs.title: PointsChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.PointsChanged: Signal<Team, number> Fires whenever a team's points value changes.

searchDocs.title: CapturingChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.CapturingChanged: Signal<Team, number> Fires whenever a team's capture progress changes.

searchDocs.title: HolderAdded​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.HolderAdded: Signal<Team > Fires when a team becomes a holder (reaches full capture).

searchDocs.title: HolderRemoved​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.HolderRemoved: Signal<Team > Fires when a team loses holder status.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal.new( name: string,-- Unique name for this instance. settings: Settings-- Round configuration. Teams and Parts are required. ) → Terminal Creates a new Terminal gamemode instance.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:Start( metadata: any?-- Forwarded to Started listeners. ) → () Starts the round. Resets all per-team state, sets up QuickZone detection, then calls GamemodeCore:Start.

searchDocs.title: Stop​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:Stop( winner: Team? ,-- The winning team, or nil for a draw. metadata: any?-- Forwarded to Stopped listeners. ) → () Stops the round, tears down QuickZone, fires Stopped, then clears all per-team state.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:Destroy() → () Destroys the Terminal instance. Cleans up all Terminal signals then calls GamemodeCore:Destroy.

searchDocs.title: mount​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:mount(fn: ( gm: Terminal, ctx: HookContext ) → ()) → () → () Attaches a reactive function to this Terminal instance. Delegates to GamemodeCore:mount.

searchDocs.title: getState​
searchDocs.content: </> Terminal:getState() → { Active: boolean, Paused: boolean, Overtime: boolean, Contested: boolean, Elapsed: number, PlayingTeams: {[Team ]: TeamInfo}, Holders: {Team }, PointsNeeded: number, CaptureTime: number, Increase: number, Rollback: number } Returns a frozen snapshot of the current round state. Useful for replicating to clients or reading state outside of a mount. local state = gm:getState() print(state.Active, state.Overtime, state.Elapsed)

searchDocs.title: Config​
searchDocs.content: </> type Config = {[string]: any} A free-form config table passed to GamemodeCore.new. Every key is copied directly onto the instance.

searchDocs.title: Started​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.Started: Signal<any?> Fires when the gamemode starts. Receives the optional metadata passed to Start.

searchDocs.title: Stopped​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.Stopped: Signal<any?,any?> Fires when the gamemode stops. Receives the winner and optional metadata passed to Stop.

searchDocs.title: OnPaused​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.OnPaused: Signal<any?> Fires when the gamemode is paused. Receives the optional metadata passed to Pause.

searchDocs.title: ElapsedChanged​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.ElapsedChanged: Signal<number> Fires every Heartbeat while the gamemode is active and unpaused, with the current elapsed time.

searchDocs.title: Destroying​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.Destroying: Signal<GamemodeCore> Fires just before the gamemode is destroyed.

searchDocs.title: register​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.register( gmType: string,-- Unique identifier for this gamemode type. tickFn: ( self: GamemodeCore, dt: number ) → ()-- Called every Heartbeat while active and unpaused. ) → () Registers a gamemode type with a tick function. Must be called beforeGamemodeCore.new for that type. Registering the same type twice overwrites the previous registration with a warning. GamemodeCore.register("MyMode", function(self, dt) if self.Elapsed > 300 then self:Stop(nil) end end)

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.new( gmType: string,-- A type previously registered with GamemodeCore.register. name: string,-- Unique name for this instance. Used by GamemodeCore.getByName. config: Config-- Key-value pairs copied onto the instance. ) → GamemodeCore Creates a new GamemodeCore instance of the given registered type. Every key in config is shallow-copied onto the instance. local gm = GamemodeCore.new("MyMode", "RoundOne", { ScoreLimit = 100, })

searchDocs.title: getByName​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore.getByName(name: string) → GamemodeCore? Returns the GamemodeCore instance registered under name, or nil if no such instance exists.

searchDocs.title: mount​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore:mount( fn: ( gm: GamemodeCore, ctx: HookContext ) → ()-- Reactive function to mount. ) → () → ()-- Call to unmount and clean up. Attaches a reactive function to the gamemode. The function receives the instance and a HookContext and is called every Heartbeat while the gamemode is active and unpaused. Returns an unmount function. Call it to detach the mount and run any pending useEffect cleanups. local unmount = gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local score, setScore = ctx:useState(0) ctx:useEffect(function() local c = self.Stopped:Connect(function() setScore(0) end) return function() c:Disconnect() end end, {}) end) -- later: unmount()

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore:Start( metadata: any?-- Optional value forwarded to Started listeners. ) → () Starts the gamemode. Transitions Active to true, resets Elapsed to 0, begins the Heartbeat loop, ticks all mounted functions once, then fires Started. Does nothing if already active.

searchDocs.title: Stop​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore:Stop( winner: any?,-- Passed as the first argument to Stopped listeners. metadata: any?-- Passed as the second argument to Stopped listeners. ) → () Stops the gamemode. Disconnects the Heartbeat loop, flushes alluseEffect cleanups, fires Stopped, then resets Elapsed to 0. Does nothing if not active.

searchDocs.title: Pause​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore:Pause( metadata: any?-- Optional value forwarded to OnPaused listeners. ) → () Pauses the gamemode, suspending the tick loop and mount calls. Fires OnPaused. Does nothing if not active or already paused.

searchDocs.title: Resume​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore:Resume() → () Resumes a paused gamemode. Does nothing if not active or not paused.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> GamemodeCore:Destroy() → () Destroys the gamemode. Fires Destroying, stops the round if active, destroys all mounts and signals, and removes the instance from the global registry. After calling this, the instance must not be used again.

searchDocs.title: Packages​
searchDocs.content: Package	Descriptiongamemode-core	Reactive hook-based gamemode lifecycle core gamemode-terminal	Terminal (control-point) gamemode built on gamemode-core  For the latest versions of each package, see their respective wally.toml files or search on wally.run.

searchDocs.title: Installation​
searchDocs.content: Add whichever packages you need to your wally.toml and run wally install:  [dependencies] GamemodeCore = "codjo3/gamemode-core@VERSION" GamemodeTerminal = "codjo3/gamemode-terminal@VERSION"

searchDocs.title: Quick Example​
searchDocs.content: local Terminal = require(Packages.GamemodeTerminal) local gm = Terminal.new("RoundOne", { Teams = { game.Teams.Red, game.Teams.Blue }, Parts = { workspace.ControlPoint }, PointsNeeded = 1200, CaptureTime = 10, }) gm.Stopped:Connect(function(winner) print(winner.Name .. " wins!") end) gm:Start()

searchDocs.title: Installation​
searchDocs.content: [dependencies] GamemodeCore = "codjo3/gamemode-core@VERSION"   wally install   Check wally.run or the module's wally.toml for the latest version.

searchDocs.title: Registering a Type​
searchDocs.content: Before creating a gamemode instance you must register a type with a tick function. The tick function is called every Heartbeat while the gamemode is active and not paused.  local GamemodeCore = require(Packages.GamemodeCore) GamemodeCore.register("MyMode", function(self, dt) -- self is the GamemodeCore instance -- dt is delta time in seconds end)

searchDocs.title: Creating an Instance​
searchDocs.content: local gm = GamemodeCore.new("MyMode", "RoundOne", { ScoreLimit = 100, })   The third argument is a config table — every key is copied directly onto the instance, so gm.ScoreLimit == 100.

searchDocs.title: Lifecycle​
searchDocs.content: gm:Start() -- transitions to Active, begins Heartbeat loop gm:Pause() -- suspends the tick loop, fires OnPaused gm:Resume() -- resumes the tick loop gm:Stop(winner) -- ends the round, fires Stopped with winner gm:Destroy() -- tears down everything, removes from registry

searchDocs.title: Signals​
searchDocs.content: Every instance exposes typed signals you can connect to:  gm.Started:Connect(function(metadata) end) gm.Stopped:Connect(function(winner, metadata) end) gm.OnPaused:Connect(function(metadata) end) gm.ElapsedChanged:Connect(function(elapsed: number) end) gm.Destroying:Connect(function(gm) end)

searchDocs.title: Hooks​
searchDocs.content: mount lets you attach reactive logic to a gamemode instance. It takes a function that receives the instance and a HookContext, and re-runs that function every heartbeat while the gamemode is active.  gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local score, setScore = ctx:useState(0) ctx:useEffect(function() -- runs once on mount, cleanup runs on unmount or when deps change local c = self.Stopped:Connect(function() setScore(0) end) return function() c:Disconnect() end end, {}) end)   mount returns an unmount function — call it to remove that mounted function and run its cleanup:  local unmount = gm:mount(function(self, ctx) ... end) -- later: unmount()

searchDocs.title: Available Hooks​
searchDocs.content: Hook	DescriptionuseState	Persistent value slot with a setter dep useReducer	Like useState but driven by a reducer function useMemo	Cached computed value, recomputed when deps change useCallback	Memoized function reference useRef	Persistent mutable ref that does not trigger re-renders useEffect	Side effect with optional cleanup, re-runs when deps change  See the API reference for full signatures.

searchDocs.title: Installation​
searchDocs.content: [dependencies] GamemodeCore = "codjo3/gamemode-core@VERSION" GamemodeTerminal = "codjo3/gamemode-terminal@VERSION"   wally install   Check wally.run or the module's wally.toml for the latest version.

searchDocs.title: Basic Usage​
searchDocs.content: local Terminal = require(Packages.GamemodeTerminal) local gm = Terminal.new("RoundOne", { Teams = { game.Teams.Red, game.Teams.Blue }, Parts = { workspace.ControlPoint }, }) gm.Stopped:Connect(function(winner) print(winner.Name .. " wins!") end) gm:Start()

searchDocs.title: Settings​
searchDocs.content: All settings are optional except Teams and Parts.  Setting	Type	Default	DescriptionTeams	{ Team }	required	Teams competing in the round Parts	{ BasePart }	required	Parts that define the capture zone(s) Increase	number	1	Points per second earned while holding Rollback	number	1	Points per second lost while not holding PointsNeeded	number	1200	Points required to win CaptureTime	number	1	Seconds to fully capture a point RequireDominanceToIncrease	boolean	true	Only earn points if the opponent has zero points ProgressWhileContested	boolean	true	Allow capture progress when both teams are in zone CaptureWhileDead	boolean	false	Count dead players toward zone occupation CaptureStacking	boolean	false	Scale capture speed by player count difference OvertimeThreshold	number	-1	Seconds before overtime starts (-1 = disabled) OvertimeIncrease	number	1	Points per second during overtime OvertimeRollback	number	1	Rollback rate during overtime OvertimePointsNeeded	number	120	Points to win during overtime OvertimeDefenderWinFullDominance	boolean	true	Defenders win if they fully capture while opponent has zero points DefendingTeam	Team?	Teams[1]	The defending team for overtime win condition

searchDocs.title: Capture & Holders​
searchDocs.content: Each team has a Capturing value (from 0 to CaptureTime). When Capturing reaches CaptureTime the team becomes a Holder and begins earning points. If they lose the zone their progress rolls back.

searchDocs.title: Points​
searchDocs.content: Holders earn Increase points per second. Non-holders lose Rollback points per second. The first team to reach PointsNeeded wins the round, calling gm:Stop(winnerTeam).

searchDocs.title: Overtime​
searchDocs.content: If OvertimeThreshold is set and the elapsed time reaches it without a winner, overtime begins. OvertimeEntered fires, and the win condition switches to OvertimePointsNeeded. A tied overtime can be broken by one team capturing the point while the other has zero points.

searchDocs.title: Signals​
searchDocs.content: gm.ContestedChanged:Connect(function(contested: boolean) end) gm.OvertimeEntered:Connect(function() end) gm.PointsChanged:Connect(function(team: Team, points: number) end) gm.CapturingChanged:Connect(function(team: Team, capturing: number) end) gm.HolderAdded:Connect(function(team: Team) end) gm.HolderRemoved:Connect(function(team: Team) end) -- Inherited from GamemodeCore: gm.Started:Connect(function(metadata) end) gm.Stopped:Connect(function(winner: Team?, metadata) end) gm.ElapsedChanged:Connect(function(elapsed: number) end)

searchDocs.title: Reading State​
searchDocs.content: getState() returns a frozen snapshot of the current round state, useful for replication:  local state = gm:getState() -- state.Active, state.Overtime, state.Contested, -- state.PlayingTeams, state.Holders, state.Elapsed, ...

searchDocs.title: Hooks​
searchDocs.content: Terminal inherits mount from GamemodeCore:  gm:mount(function(self, ctx) local holders, setHolders = ctx:useState({}) ctx:useEffect(function() local a = self.HolderAdded:Connect(function(team) setHolders(table.clone(self.Holders)) end) local r = self.HolderRemoved:Connect(function(team) setHolders(table.clone(self.Holders)) end) return function() a:Disconnect() r:Disconnect() end end, {}) end)
